{{神社 |名称 = 守山八幡宮 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=伊豆の国市}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|2|45.7|N|138|56|18.6|E}}}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[伊豆の国市]]寺家1204-1[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402097 守山八幡宮] - 静岡県神社庁 |位置 = {{Coord|35|2|45.7|N|138|56|18.6|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[誉田別命]]、[[大山祇神]]、[[木花開耶姫命]] |社格 = [[伊豆国の式内社一覧|式内社]]論社
旧[[村社]] |創建 = [[647年]]（[[大化]]3年） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月10日付近の日曜 |神事 = }} '''守山八幡宮'''（もりやまはちまんぐう）は、[[静岡県]][[伊豆の国市]]にある[[神社]]。[[伊豆国の式内社一覧|式内社]]である石徳高(いわとこたけ)神社の論社であり、[[源頼朝]]が挙兵に当たり平家追討祈願をした神社としても知られる境内由緒書き[https://izunotabi.com/sightseeing/moriyama-hachimangu/ 守山八幡宮] - 伊豆の国市観光協会[https://www.surugawan.net/guide/333.html 守山八幡宮] - 伊豆駿河観光ガイド。 == 概要 == 伊豆の国市（旧[[韮山町]]）の寺家（じけ）地区の西部、[[願成就院]]をはじめとして周辺に[[北条家]]・[[源頼朝]]関連施設が集中する守山（もりやま）の山中に鎮座している。 境内は2段構成になっており、石段を上がってすぐの場所には舞殿・拝殿があり、そこから更に急な石段142段を上がった先に、本殿・拝殿がある。下の舞殿では、毎年10月の例大祭でしゃぎり・[[三番叟]]が奉納される[https://www.city.izunokuni.shizuoka.jp/tosikei/documents/2-7nirayama-oohito.pdf 韮山・大仁地域の神社の祭礼と三番叟にみる歴史的風致] - 伊豆の国市 都市計画課。 == 歴史 == 創建は[[647年]]（[[大化]]3年）であり、[[大山祇神]]を祭神とし、後に[[伊豆国の式内社一覧|式内社]]となる石徳高神社であったとされる。 [[907年]]（[[延喜]]7年）、[[豊前国]][[宇佐神宮]]より[[八幡神]]を勧請・合祀して守山八幡となる。 [[1180年]]（[[治承]]4年）8月、源頼朝が平家追討を祈願して挙兵、配下数十の兵が（後の[[韮山代官所]]に程近い）[[山木兼隆]]の館を襲撃、火煙が上がるのをこの地から眺めていたとされる。 現在の本殿は、[[1632年]]（[[寛永]]9年）に[[久能城]]主・[[榊原照久]]によって造営されたものである。 == 祭神 == * [[誉田別命]] * [[大山祇神]] * [[木花開耶姫命]] == 境内 == * 本殿・拝殿 * 舞殿・拝殿 * 源頼朝挙兵之碑 * 鳥居 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:もりやまはちまんくう}} [[Category:伊豆の国市の歴史]] [[Category:伊豆の国市の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:八幡宮]]

旧[[村社]] |創建 = [[647年]]（[[大化]]3年） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月10日付近の日曜 |神事 = }} '''守山八幡宮'''（もりやまはちまんぐう）は、[[静岡県]][[伊豆の国市]]にある[[神社]]。[[伊豆国の式内社一覧|式内社]]である石徳高(いわとこたけ)神社の論社であり、[[源頼朝]]が挙兵に当たり平家追討祈願をした神社としても知られる境内由緒書き[https://izunotabi.com/sightseeing/moriyama-hachimangu/ 守山八幡宮] - 伊豆の国市観光協会[https://www.surugawan.net/guide/333.html 守山八幡宮] - 伊豆駿河観光ガイド。 == 概要 == 伊豆の国市（旧[[韮山町]]）の寺家（じけ）地区の西部、[[願成就院]]をはじめとして周辺に[[北条家]]・[[源頼朝]]関連施設が集中する守山（もりやま）の山中に鎮座している。 境内は2段構成になっており、石段を上がってすぐの場所には舞殿・拝殿があり、そこから更に急な石段142段を上がった先に、本殿・拝殿がある。下の舞殿では、毎年10月の例大祭でしゃぎり・[[三番叟]]が奉納される[https://www.city.izunokuni.shizuoka.jp/tosikei/documents/2-7nirayama-oohito.pdf 韮山・大仁地域の神社の祭礼と三番叟にみる歴史的風致] - 伊豆の国市 都市計画課。 == 歴史 == 創建は[[647年]]（[[大化]]3年）であり、[[大山祇神]]を祭神とし、後に[[伊豆国の式内社一覧|式内社]]となる石徳高神社であったとされる。 [[907年]]（[[延喜]]7年）、[[豊前国]][[宇佐神宮]]より[[八幡神]]を勧請・合祀して守山八幡となる。 [[1180年]]（[[治承]]4年）8月、源頼朝が平家追討を祈願して挙兵、配下数十の兵が（後の[[韮山代官所]]に程近い）[[山木兼隆]]の館を襲撃、火煙が上がるのをこの地から眺めていたとされる。 現在の本殿は、[[1632年]]（[[寛永]]9年）に[[久能城]]主・[[榊原照久]]によって造営されたものとされる[https://www.city.izunokuni.shizuoka.jp/tosikei/documents/2-4houjounosato-huuti.pdf 北条の里と旧下田街道に見る歴史的風致] - 伊豆の国市 都市計画課。（それ以前のものとしては、[[韮山城]]主・[[内藤信成]]による[[1597年]]（[[慶長]]2年）の[[棟札]]も伝わっている。） == 祭神 == * [[誉田別命]] * [[大山祇神]] * [[木花開耶姫命]] == 境内 == * 本殿・拝殿 * 舞殿・拝殿 * 源頼朝挙兵之碑 * 鳥居 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:もりやまはちまんくう}} [[Category:伊豆の国市の歴史]] [[Category:伊豆の国市の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:八幡宮]]

旧[[村社]] |創建 = [[647年]]（[[大化]]3年） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月10日付近の日曜 |神事 = }} '''守山八幡宮'''（もりやまはちまんぐう）は、[[静岡県]][[伊豆の国市]]にある[[神社]]。[[伊豆国の式内社一覧|式内社]]である石徳高(いわとこたけ)神社の論社であり、[[源頼朝]]が挙兵に当たり平家追討祈願をした神社としても知られる境内由緒書き[https://izunotabi.com/sightseeing/moriyama-hachimangu/ 守山八幡宮] - 伊豆の国市観光協会[https://www.surugawan.net/guide/333.html 守山八幡宮] - 伊豆駿河観光ガイド。 == 概要 == 伊豆の国市（旧[[韮山町]]）の寺家（じけ）地区の西部、[[願成就院]]をはじめとして周辺に[[北条家]]・[[源頼朝]]関連施設が集中する守山（もりやま）の山中に鎮座している。 境内は2段構成になっており、石段を上がってすぐの場所には舞殿・拝殿があり、そこから更に急な石段142段を上がった先に、本殿・拝殿がある。下の舞殿では、毎年10月の例大祭でしゃぎり・[[三番叟]]が奉納される[https://www.city.izunokuni.shizuoka.jp/tosikei/documents/nisyu32.pdf 守山八幡宮と例大祭] - 伊豆の国市 都市計画課[https://www.city.izunokuni.shizuoka.jp/tosikei/documents/2-7nirayama-oohito.pdf 韮山・大仁地域の神社の祭礼と三番叟にみる歴史的風致] - 伊豆の国市 都市計画課。 == 歴史 == 創建は[[647年]]（[[大化]]3年）であり、[[大山祇神]]を祭神とし、後に[[伊豆国の式内社一覧|式内社]]となる石徳高神社であったとされる。 [[907年]]（[[延喜]]7年）、[[豊前国]][[宇佐神宮]]より[[八幡神]]を勧請・合祀して守山八幡となる。 [[1180年]]（[[治承]]4年）8月、源頼朝が平家追討を祈願して挙兵、配下数十の兵が（後の[[韮山代官所]]に程近い）[[山木兼隆]]の館を襲撃、火煙が上がるのをこの地から眺めていたとされる。 現在の本殿は、[[1632年]]（[[寛永]]9年）に[[久能城]]主・[[榊原照久]]によって造営されたものとされる[https://www.city.izunokuni.shizuoka.jp/tosikei/documents/2-4houjounosato-huuti.pdf 北条の里と旧下田街道に見る歴史的風致] - 伊豆の国市 都市計画課。（それ以前のものとしては、[[韮山城]]主・[[内藤信成]]による[[1597年]]（[[慶長]]2年）の[[棟札]]も伝わっている。） 舞殿は、[[1934年]]（[[昭和]]9年）に建てられた。 == 祭神 == * [[誉田別命]] * [[大山祇神]] * [[木花開耶姫命]] == 境内 == * 本殿・拝殿 * 舞殿・拝殿 * 源頼朝挙兵之碑 * 鳥居 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:もりやまはちまんくう}} [[Category:伊豆の国市の歴史]] [[Category:伊豆の国市の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:八幡宮]]

旧[[村社]] |創建 = [[647年]]（[[大化]]3年） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月10日付近の日曜 |神事 = }} '''守山八幡宮'''（もりやまはちまんぐう）は、[[静岡県]][[伊豆の国市]]にある[[神社]]。[[狩野川]]を挟んだ西方・江間地区にある[[豆塚神社]]と共に、[[伊豆国の式内社一覧|式内社]]である'''石徳高神社'''（いわとこたけ）の後継神社の1つであり、[[源頼朝]]が挙兵に当たり平家追討祈願をした神社としても知られる境内由緒書き[https://izunotabi.com/sightseeing/moriyama-hachimangu/ 守山八幡宮] - 伊豆の国市観光協会[https://www.surugawan.net/guide/333.html 守山八幡宮] - 伊豆駿河観光ガイド。 == 概要 == 伊豆の国市（旧[[韮山町]]）の寺家（じけ）地区の西部、[[願成就院]]をはじめとして周辺に[[北条家]]・[[源頼朝]]関連施設が集中する守山（もりやま）の山中に鎮座している。 境内は2段構成になっており、石段を上がってすぐの場所には舞殿・拝殿があり、そこから更に急な石段142段を上がった先に、本殿・拝殿がある。下の舞殿では、毎年10月の例大祭でしゃぎり・[[三番叟]]が奉納される[https://www.city.izunokuni.shizuoka.jp/tosikei/documents/nisyu32.pdf 守山八幡宮と例大祭] - 伊豆の国市 都市計画課[https://www.city.izunokuni.shizuoka.jp/tosikei/documents/2-7nirayama-oohito.pdf 韮山・大仁地域の神社の祭礼と三番叟にみる歴史的風致] - 伊豆の国市 都市計画課。 == 歴史 == 創建は[[647年]]（[[大化]]3年）であり、[[大山祇神]]を祭神とし、後に[[伊豆国の式内社一覧|式内社]]となる石徳高神社であったとされる。 [[907年]]（[[延喜]]7年）、[[豊前国]][[宇佐神宮]]より[[八幡神]]を勧請・合祀し、守山への遷座を経て守山八幡となる。 [[1180年]]（[[治承]]4年）8月、源頼朝が平家追討を祈願して挙兵、配下数十の兵が（後の[[韮山代官所]]に程近い）[[山木兼隆]]の館を襲撃、火煙が上がるのをこの地から眺めていたとされる。 現在の本殿は、[[1632年]]（[[寛永]]9年）に[[久能城]]主・[[榊原照久]]によって造営されたものとされる[https://www.city.izunokuni.shizuoka.jp/tosikei/documents/2-4houjounosato-huuti.pdf 北条の里と旧下田街道に見る歴史的風致] - 伊豆の国市 都市計画課。（それ以前のものとしては、[[韮山城]]主・[[内藤信成]]による[[1597年]]（[[慶長]]2年）の[[棟札]]も伝わっている。） 舞殿は、[[1934年]]（[[昭和]]9年）に建てられた。 == 祭神 == * [[誉田別命]] * [[大山祇神]] * [[木花開耶姫命]] == 境内 == * 本殿・拝殿 * 舞殿・拝殿 * 源頼朝挙兵之碑 * 鳥居 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:もりやまはちまんくう}} [[Category:伊豆の国市の歴史]] [[Category:伊豆の国市の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:八幡宮]]

旧[[村社]] |創建 = [[647年]]（[[大化]]3年） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月10日付近の日曜 |神事 = }} '''守山八幡宮'''（もりやまはちまんぐう）は、[[静岡県]][[伊豆の国市]]にある[[神社]]。[[狩野川]]を挟んだ西方・江間地区にある[[豆塚神社]]と共に、[[伊豆国の式内社一覧|式内社]]である'''石徳高神社'''（いわとこたけじんじゃ）の後継神社の1つであり、[[源頼朝]]が挙兵に当たり平家追討祈願をした神社としても知られる境内由緒書き[https://izunotabi.com/sightseeing/moriyama-hachimangu/ 守山八幡宮] - 伊豆の国市観光協会[https://www.surugawan.net/guide/333.html 守山八幡宮] - 伊豆駿河観光ガイド。 == 概要 == 伊豆の国市（旧[[韮山町]]）の寺家（じけ）地区の西部、[[願成就院]]をはじめとして周辺に[[北条家]]・[[源頼朝]]関連施設や、室町時代の[[伝堀越御所跡]]などが集中する守山（もりやま）の山中に鎮座している。 境内は2段構成になっており、石段を上がってすぐの場所には舞殿・拝殿があり、そこから更に急な石段142段を上がった先に、本殿・拝殿がある。下の舞殿では、毎年10月の例大祭でしゃぎり・[[三番叟]]が奉納される[https://www.city.izunokuni.shizuoka.jp/tosikei/documents/nisyu32.pdf 守山八幡宮と例大祭] - 伊豆の国市 都市計画課[https://www.city.izunokuni.shizuoka.jp/tosikei/documents/2-7nirayama-oohito.pdf 韮山・大仁地域の神社の祭礼と三番叟にみる歴史的風致] - 伊豆の国市 都市計画課。 == 歴史 == 創建は[[647年]]（[[大化]]3年）であり、[[大山祇神]]を祭神とし、後に[[伊豆国の式内社一覧|式内社]]となる石徳高神社であったとされる。 [[907年]]（[[延喜]]7年）、[[豊前国]][[宇佐神宮]]より[[八幡神]]を勧請・合祀し、守山への遷座を経て守山八幡となる。 [[1180年]]（[[治承]]4年）8月、源頼朝が平家追討を祈願して挙兵、配下数十の兵が（後の[[韮山代官所]]に程近い）[[山木兼隆]]の館を襲撃、火煙が上がるのをこの地から眺めていたとされる。 現在の本殿は、[[1632年]]（[[寛永]]9年）に[[久能城]]主・[[榊原照久]]によって造営されたものとされる[https://www.city.izunokuni.shizuoka.jp/tosikei/documents/2-4houjounosato-huuti.pdf 北条の里と旧下田街道に見る歴史的風致] - 伊豆の国市 都市計画課。（それ以前のものとしては、[[韮山城]]主・[[内藤信成]]による[[1597年]]（[[慶長]]2年）の[[棟札]]も伝わっている。） 舞殿は、[[1934年]]（[[昭和]]9年）に建てられた。 == 祭神 == * [[誉田別命]] * [[大山祇神]] * [[木花開耶姫命]] == 境内 == * 本殿・拝殿 * 舞殿・拝殿 * 源頼朝挙兵之碑 * 鳥居 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == * {{Commonscat-inline|Moriyama-hachimangu (Izunokuni)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:もりやまはちまんくう}} [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:伊豆の国市の歴史]] [[Category:伊豆の国市の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:八幡宮]]

[[Category:new pages]][[ja:守山八幡宮 (伊豆の国市)]] ``` {{TOCright}} '''Nenbutsu-ji''' (念佛寺, ねんぶつじ) == Temples == There are temples with the same name located in various regions. === Chūbu Region === * {{ill|Nenbutsu-ji (Niigata City)|ja|念佛寺 (新潟市)}} – A temple of the {{ill|Jishū Ōtani-ha|ja|真宗大谷派}} located in Chūō Ward, Niigata City, in the 1st chōme of {{ill|Sekiyahonmura-machi|ja|関屋本村町}}. It is also known as Saitoyama Myōkōin Nenbutsu-ji. * {{ill|Nenbutsu-ji (Kiyosu City)|ja|念佛寺 (清須市)}} (also known as {{ill|Kamegaikeyama Hōkokujii|ja|龜嶽山寶國寺}}) – A temple of the {{ill|Jōdo Sect Saizan Zenrin-ji-ha|ja|浄土宗西山禅林寺派}} located in Odai 1-chōme, Nishibiwajima-cho, Kiyosu City, Aichi Prefecture. === Kinki Region === * {{ill|Adashin Nenbutsu-ji|ja|化野念佛寺}} (read as Adashin Nenbutsu-ji) – A temple of the {{ill|Jōdo Sect Chinzai-ha|ja|浄土宗鎮西派}} located in Torii-hon, Adashin-cho, Saga, Ukyo Ward, Kyoto City. Also known as Kasei-san Tōzenin Adashin Nenbutsu-ji. * {{ill|Atago Nenbutsu-ji|ja|愛宕念佛寺}} (read as Otagi Nenbutsu-ji) – A Tendai temple located in Fukaya-cho, Torii-hon, Ukyo Ward, Kyoto City. It is also known as Tōkaku-san Atago Nenbutsu-ji. * {{ill|Mizugokuyō-ji|ja|水子供養寺}} (also known as {{ill|Nenbutsu-ji (Kyoto, Ukyo Ward)|ja|念佛寺 (京都市右京区)}}) – A temple located in Miyamae-cho, Yamanochi, Ukyo Ward, Kyoto City. * {{ill|Ohara Nenbutsu-ji|ja|大原念佛寺}} – A Tendai temple located in Raigēin-cho, Ōhara, Sakyo Ward, Kyoto City. Also known as Roritsusan Nenbutsu-ji, founded by 【{{ill|Goryō Shinnō|ja|護良親王}}】 (associated with both Ōtō Shrine and Son'un Hōshinnō). * {{ill|Dainen Nenbutsu-ji|ja|大念仏寺}} – The head temple of the {{ill|Yūzū Nenbutsu Sect|ja|融通念佛宗}}, located in Hirano-ku, Osaka City at Hirano Kamimachi. * {{ill|Nenbutsu-ji (Osaka City)|ja|念佛寺 (大阪市)}} – A Jōdo temple located in Uehonmachi 4-chōme, Tennōji Ward, Osaka City, also known as Mururyōzan Sesshuin Nenbutsu-ji.<ref>{{Cite web |title=念佛寺本堂 文化遺産オンライン |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/147155 |website=bunka.nii.ac.jp |access-date=2023-12-05 |language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=念佛寺本堂 – 大阪文化財ナビ |url=https://osaka-bunkazainavi.org/bunkazai/%E5%BF%B5%E4%BB%8F%E5%AF%BA%E6%9C%AC%E5%A0%82 |access-date=2023-12-05 |language=ja}}</ref> * {{ill|Nenbutsu-ji (Sakai City)|ja|念佛寺 (堺市)}} – A Jōdo temple located in Higashi, Ishizutsumachi, Hamadera, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture. Also known as Nyūokayama Sesshuin Nenbutsu-ji.<ref>{{Cite web |title=乳岡山念佛寺 |url=http://izumisaigoku.web.fc2.com/nenbutsuji.html |website=izumisaigoku.web.fc2.com |access-date=2023-12-05}}</ref> * {{ill|Nenbutsu-ji (Yao City)|ja|念佛寺 (八尾市)}} – A temple of the {{ill|Yūzū Nenbutsu Sect|ja|融通念佛宗}} located in Kyūhōji 3-chōme, Yao City, Osaka. * {{ill|Nenbutsu-ji (Nara City)|ja|念佛寺 (奈良市)}} – A Jōdo temple located in Hankoku-cho, Nara City, Nara Prefecture. Also known as Kōmyōzan Sesshuin Nenbutsu-ji. The seated statue of Amida Nyorai, the main image, is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan. It is listed as the first temple in the Iga 25 Sacred Sites and holds the number one in the Jōdo sect Iga Diocese.<ref>{{Cite web |title=浄土宗 光明山 攝取院 念佛寺 |url=http://www.ict.ne.jp/~nenbutu/ |website=www.ict.ne.jp |access-date=2023-12-05}}</ref> * {{ill|Nenbutsu-ji (Gojo City)|ja|念佛寺 (五條市)}} – A temple of the {{ill|Koyasan Shingon Sect|ja|高野山真言宗}} located in Ōtsu-cho, Gojo City, Nara Prefecture, famous for its "Oni-hashiri" (Demon Dash) at the Dada-do hall of Nenbutsu-ji. * {{ill|Nenbutsu-ji (Kobe City)|ja|念佛寺 (神戸市)}} – A Jōdo temple located in Arima-cho, Kita Ward, Kobe City, Hyōgo Prefecture, part of the Arima Onsen area, known as Kōmyōzan Sesshuin Nenbutsu-ji. * {{ill|Nenbutsu-ji (Iga City)|ja|念佛寺 (伊賀市)}} – A Jōdo temple located in Ueno, Teramachi, Iga City, Mie Prefecture. It is known as Kōmyōzan Sesshuin Nenbutsu-ji; its principal image, a seated Amida Nyorai, is designated as an Important Cultural Property. It is the first temple in the Iga 25 Sacred Sites and is numbered as the first in the Jōdo sect Iga Diocese.<ref>{{Cite web |title=浄土宗 光明山 攝取院 念佛寺 |url=http://www.ict.ne.jp/~nenbutu/ |website=www.ict.ne.jp |access-date=2023-12-05}}</ref>
== Place Names == * In Nagano Prefecture, within Nagano City, Nakajō, Nikkasuno, there is a Nenbutsu-ji (念佛寺) associated with the former Minonai District Nenbutsu-ji Village, the former Kamisuin District, and Nikkasuno Nenbutsu-ji of Narimura. * Nenbutsu-ji Sawatake == Related Topics == * {{ill|Nenbutsu River|ja|念佛川}} * {{ill|Nenbutsu-in|ja|念佛院}} * {{ill|Nenbutsu-yama|ja|念佛山}} == Footnotes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}

[[Category:new pages]][[ja:守山八幡宮 (伊豆の国市)]] ``` {{Shinto shrine | name = Moriyama Hachimangu | image = [[File:Moriyama-hachimangu (Izunokuni), torii-1.jpg|250px]]
Torii Gate (with the monument marking Minamoto no Yoritomo’s raising of arms to fight the Taira clan)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|2|45.7|N|138|56|18.6|E}}}} | location = 1204-1 Jike, Izu-no-Kuni City, Shizuoka Prefecture [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402097 Moriyama Hachimangu] - Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Association | coordinates = {{Coord|35|2|45.7|N|138|56|18.6|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Hondawakemiko]], [[Oyamatsumiko]], [[Konohanasakuya-hime]] | shrine rank = [[Shikinaisha|Shikinai Shrine]]
Former [[Mura Shrine]] | founded = 647 (3rd year of Taika) | main hall = | alt names = | pilgrimage = | annual festival = Sunday around October 10th | event rituals = | map = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Moriyama Hachimangu|coordinates= {{coord2|35|2|45.7|N|138|56|18.6|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Moriyama Hachimangu''' (守山八幡宮) is a Shinto shrine located in Izu-no-Kuni City, Shizuoka Prefecture, Japan. Along with [[Mamezuka Shrine]] located in the nearby [[Enma]] district to the west of the [[Kano River]], it is one of the successor shrines of the [[Iwatokotake Shrine]] in [[Izu Province]] (modern-day Shizuoka Prefecture), listed as a shrine in historical documents. == Overview == Moriyama Hachimangu is nestled within the mountains of Moriyama (守山) in the western part of Jike district of what is now Izu-no-Kuni city (formerly Nira Town). Surrounding areas include several historical sites associated with the [[Hojo family]] and the commander [[Minamoto no Yoritomo]], including the [[Ganjojuin Temple]] and remnants of the [[Muromachi period]] [[Horiuchi Palace]]. The shrine steps up in two stages. The first stage leads directly to the dance stage (Mai-den) and a worship hall (Haiden), while ascension of a further 142 steps brings one to the main hall (Hon-den) and another worship hall (Haiden). Annually, traditional performances of the "Shagiri" and "Sanbansō" are performed during the grand autumn festival held around October 10th.[https://izunotabi.com/sightseeing/moriyama-hachimangu/ Moriyama Hachimangu] - Izu-no-kuni City Tourism Association[https://www.surugawan.net/guide/333.html Moriyama Hachimangu] - Suruga Izu Kanto Tourism Guide == History == The shrine’s establishment is believed to have occurred in 647 AD (3rd year of Taika), originally dedicated to [[Oyamatsumiko]]. Later, in 907 AD, it was redesignated with the additional deity [[Hachiman]], brought from [[Usa Jingu]] in [[Bizen Province]], thus becoming known as Moriyama Hachimangu.temple heritage record[https://izunotabi.com/sightseeing/moriyama-hachimangu/ Moriyama Hachimangu] - Izu-no-kuni City Tourism Association In 1180 AD (4th year of the Jisho era), [[Minamoto no Yoritomo]] raised his arms in prayer to Hachimangu as he prepared to launch an attack on the Taira army. From this location, he is believed to have witnessed the smoke rising from the burning of [[Yamaki Kanetaka]]'s mansion, which was the first military engagement in his struggle against the Taira. Today's main hall was constructed in 1632 AD (9th year of the Kan'ei era) by the [[Kunojo Castle]] lord [[Sakakibara Terukazu]]. There are also records of a wooden plaque dating back to 1597 AD (2nd year of the Keicho era) that acknowledge the contributions by the earlier [[Nirayama Castle]] lord, [[Naito Nobunari]].[https://www.city.izunokuni.shizuoka.jp/tosikei/documents/2-4houjounosato-huuti.pdf Hojo Village and the Former Shimoda Road Historical Landscape] - Izu-no-Kuni City, Urban Planning Division The dance stage was constructed in 1934 AD with its distinct features.[https://www.city.izunokuni.shizuoka.jp/tosikei/documents/nisyu32.pdf Moriyama Hachimangu Grand Festival] - Izu-no-Kuni City Urban Planning Division == Deity == * [[Hondawakemiko]] * [[Oyamatsumiko]] * [[Konohanasakuya-hime]] == Grounds == * Main Hall and Worship Hall * Dance Stage and Worship Hall * Monument to Minamoto no Yoritomo’s Raising of Arms * Torii Gate == Footnotes == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Moriyama-hachimangu (Izunokuni)}} * [https://izunotabi.com/sightseeing/moriyama-hachimangu/ Moriyama Hachimangu] - Izu-no-Kuni City Tourism Association {{DEFAULTSORT:モリヤマハチマン}} [[Category:Shizuoka Prefecture Shrines]] [[Category:Izu-no-Kuni City History]] [[Category:Buildings in Izu-no-Kuni City]] [[Category:Shikinai Shrines]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] ``` for 1m 2s

revision.text:
[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:守山八幡宮 (伊豆の国市)]] {{神社 |名称 = {{ill|Moriyama Hachimangu|ja|守山八幡宮}} |image = {{ill|File:Moriyama-hachimangu (Izunokuni), torii-1.jpg|ja|守山八幡宮 鳥居（左に源頼朝挙兵之碑）|lt=Moriyama Hachimangu|lt_ja=守山八幡宮}}
Torii (with the monument of {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}’s military expedition on the left)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|2|45.7|N|138|56|18.6|E}}}} |所在地 = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}{{ill|Izunokuni City|ja|伊豆の国市}}, Terake 1204-1[{{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|守山八幡宮}}] - {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Agency|ja|静岡県神社庁}} |位置 = {{Coord|35|2|45.7|N|138|56|18.6|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|Hota Wakabetsu-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命}}, {{ill|Ōyama Tsumi-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神}}, {{ill|Konohananosakuyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|木花開耶姫命}} |社格 = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}
Former Village Shrine (旧{{ill|Murasya|ja|村社}}) |創建 = {{ill|647 AD|ja|647年}} (3rd year of {{ill|Taika|ja|大化}}) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = A Sunday near {{ill|October|ja|10月}} 10 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label={{ill|Moriyama Hachimangu|ja|守山八幡宮}}|coordinates= {{coord2|35|2|45.7|N|138|56|18.6|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark={{ill|Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|ja|鳥居}}|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Moriyama Hachimangu''' (守山八幡宮, もりやまはちまんぐう) is a Shinto shrine located in Izunokuni City, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. It is one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} of Izu Province and a successor shrine of {{ill|Iwatokotake Shrine|ja|石徳高神社}} (also read as {{ill|Iwato Kotakejinja|ja|いわとこたけじんじゃ}}) along with {{ill|Mugatsuka Shrine|ja|豆塚神社}}, which is situated in the western, Emma district across the {{ill|Kano River|ja|狩野川}}. It is also known as the shrine where {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} rallied his forces and prayed for the defeat of the Taira clan.(Shrine legend document)[https://izunotabi.com/sightseeing/moriyama-hachimangu/ Moriyama Hachimangu] - {{ill|Izunokuni City Tourism Association|ja|伊豆の国市観光協会}}[https://www.surugawan.net/guide/333.html Moriyama Hachimangu] - {{ill|Izu Suruga Tourism Guide|ja|伊豆駿河観光ガイド}} == Overview == The shrine is located in the mountainous area of Moriyama, in the western part of the Terake district (formerly known as {{ill|Nirayama Town|ja|韮山町}}) of Izunokuni City. This area is home to a concentration of historical sites related to the {{ill|Hōjō family|ja|北条家}} and {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}, as well as remains of the 【伝堀越御所跡】 from the Muromachi period. The shrine grounds are arranged in two levels. Immediately after ascending a flight of stone steps, one encounters the stage hall and the haiden. Beyond these, an additional 142 steep stone steps lead to the main hall and another haiden. At the lower stage hall, during the annual grand festival in October, rituals such as the Shagiri and the offering of the "Sambansō" are performed.[https://www.city.izunokuni.shizuoka.jp/tosikei/documents/nisyu32.pdf Moriyama Hachimangu and the Grand Festival] - Izunokuni City Urban Planning Division[https://www.city.izunokuni.shizuoka.jp/tosikei/documents/2-7nirayama-oohito.pdf Historical Aesthetics of Festival Rituals and Sambansō in the Nirayama-Oohito Area] - Izunokuni City Urban Planning Division == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|647 AD|ja|647年}} (the 3rd year of {{ill|Taika|ja|大化}}), enshrining {{ill|Ōyama Tsumi-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神}}. It later became a Shikinaisha of Izu Province through its association with Iwatokotake Shrine. In {{ill|907 AD|ja|907年}} (the 7th year of {{ill|Engi|ja|延喜}}), the deity {{ill|Hachimangu|ja|八幡神}} was enshrined by invitation from {{ill|Usa Shrine|ja|宇佐神宮}} of Buzen Province and merged with the shrine, which then became known as Moriyama Hachimangu. In August {{ill|1180 AD|ja|1180年}} (the 4th year of {{ill|Jishō|ja|治承}}), when {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} prayed for the defeat of the Taira clan and rallied his forces, several dozen of his soldiers attacked the mansion of {{ill|Yamaki Kanetaka|ja|山木兼隆}}, located near what would later become the Nirayama Daikan-sho, and the ensuing flames were observed from this site. The current main hall was constructed in {{ill|1632 AD|ja|1632年}} (the 9th year of {{ill|Kannen|ja|寛永}}) by Sakakibara Teruhisa, the lord of {{ill|Kunō Castle|ja|久能城}}, and replaced an earlier structure from 1597 (the 2nd year of {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}}) by the lord of Nirayama Castle, Naitō Nobunari.[https://www.city.izunokuni.shizuoka.jp/tosikei/documents/2-4houjounosato-huuti.pdf Historical Aesthetics along the Old Shimoda Kaidō, as seen in Izunokuni City] - Izunokuni City Urban Planning Division The stage hall was constructed in {{ill|1934 AD|ja|1934年}} (Showa 9). == Deities == * {{ill|Hota Wakabetsu-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命}} * {{ill|Ōyama Tsumi-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神}} * {{ill|Konohananosakuyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|木花開耶姫命}} == Grounds == The shrine grounds include: * Main Hall and Haiden * Stage Hall and its accompanying Haiden * A monument commemorating {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}’s military expedition * Torii == Footnotes == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Moriyama-hachimangu (Izunokuni)}} * [https://izunotabi.com/sightseeing/moriyama-hachimangu/ {{ill|Moriyama Hachimangu|ja|守山八幡宮}}] - {{ill|Izunokuni City Tourism Association|ja|伊豆の国市観光協会}} * [https://www.surugawan.net/guide/333.html {{ill|Moriyama Hachimangu|ja|守山八幡宮}}] - {{ill|Izu Suruga Tourism Guide|ja|伊豆駿河観光ガイド}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:もりやまはちまんくう}} [[Category:Shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Izunokuni City]] [[Category:Architectural Structures in Izunokuni City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Izu Province]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachimangu]] [[ja:守山八幡宮]]

It is one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} of Izu Province and a successor shrine of {{ill|Iwatokotake Shrine|ja|石徳高神社}} (also read as {{ill|Iwato Kotakejinja|ja|いわとこたけじんじゃ}}) along with {{ill|Mugatsuka Shrine|ja|豆塚神社}}, which is situated in the western, Emma district across the {{ill|Kano River|ja|狩野川}}. It is also known as the shrine where {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} rallied his forces and prayed for the defeat of the Taira clan.(Shrine legend document)[https://izunotabi.com/sightseeing/moriyama-hachimangu/ Moriyama Hachimangu] - {{ill|Izunokuni City Tourism Association|ja|伊豆の国市観光協会}}[https://www.surugawan.net/guide/333.html Moriyama Hachimangu] - {{ill|Izu Suruga Tourism Guide|ja|伊豆駿河観光ガイド}} == Overview == The shrine is located in the mountainous area of Moriyama, in the western part of the Terake district (formerly known as {{ill|Nirayama Town|ja|韮山町}}) of Izunokuni City. This area is home to a concentration of historical sites related to the {{ill|Hōjō family|ja|北条家}} and {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}, as well as remains of the 【伝堀越御所跡】 from the Muromachi period. The shrine grounds are arranged in two levels. Immediately after ascending a flight of stone steps, one encounters the stage hall and the haiden. Beyond these, an additional 142 steep stone steps lead to the main hall and another haiden. At the lower stage hall, during the annual grand festival in October, rituals such as the Shagiri and the offering of the "Sambansō" are performed.[https://www.city.izunokuni.shizuoka.jp/tosikei/documents/nisyu32.pdf Moriyama Hachimangu and the Grand Festival] - Izunokuni City Urban Planning Division[https://www.city.izunokuni.shizuoka.jp/tosikei/documents/2-7nirayama-oohito.pdf Historical Aesthetics of Festival Rituals and Sambansō in the Nirayama-Oohito Area] - Izunokuni City Urban Planning Division == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|647 AD|ja|647th year }} (the 3rd year of {{ill|Taika|ja|大化}}), enshrining {{ill|Ōyama Tsumi-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神}}. In {{ill|907 AD|ja|907th year }} (the 7th year of {{ill|Engi|ja|延喜}}), the deity {{ill|Hachimangu|ja|八幡神}} was enshrined by invitation from {{ill|Usa Shrine|ja|宇佐神宮}} of Buzen Province and merged with the shrine, which then became known as Moriyama Hachimangu. The current main hall was constructed in {{ill|1632 AD|ja|1632nd year }} (the 9th year of {{ill|Kannen|ja|寛永}}) by Sakakibara Teruhisa, the lord of {{ill|Kunō Castle|ja|久能城}}, and replaced an earlier structure from 1597 (the 2nd year of {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}}) by the lord of Nirayama Castle, Naitō Nobunari.[https://www.city.izunokuni.shizuoka.jp/tosikei/documents/2-4houjounosato-huuti.pdf Historical Aesthetics along the Old Shimoda Kaidō, as seen in Izunokuni City] - Izunokuni City Urban Planning Division The stage hall was constructed in {{ill|1934 AD|ja|1934th year }} (Showa 9). == Deities == * {{ill|Hota Wakabetsu-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命}} * {{ill|Ōyama Tsumi-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神}} * {{ill|Konohananosakuyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} == Grounds == The shrine grounds include: * Main Hall and Haiden * Stage Hall and its accompanying Haiden * A monument commemorating {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}’s military expedition * Torii == Footnotes == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Moriyama-hachimangu (Izunokuni)}} * [https://izunotabi.com/sightseeing/moriyama-hachimangu/] - {{ill|Izunokuni City Tourism Association|ja|伊豆の国市観光協会}} * [https://www.surugawan.net/guide/333.html] - {{ill|Izu Suruga Tourism Guide|ja|伊豆駿河観光ガイド}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:もりやまはちまんくう}} [[Category:Shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Izunokuni City]] [[Category:Architectural Structures in Izunokuni City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Izu Province]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachimangu]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Moriyama Hachimangu (Izunokuni City)]][[Category:11]] [[ja:守山八幡宮]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Moriyama Hachimangu (Izunokuni City)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Moriyama Hachimangu (Izunokuni City)}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Moriyama Hachimangu (Izunokuni City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|2|45.7|N|138|56|18.6|E}}}} |所在地 = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuoka Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Izunokuni City|ja|伊豆の国市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunokuni City|qq=}}, Terake 1204-1[{{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|守山八幡宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine|qq=}}] - {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Agency|ja|静岡県神社庁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Agency|qq=}} |位置 = {{Coord|35|2|45.7|N|138|56|18.6|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|Hota Wakabetsu-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hota Wakabetsu-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōyama Tsumi-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyama Tsumi-no-Kami|qq=}}, {{ill|Konohananosakuyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohananosakuyahime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}} |社格 = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}
Former Village Shrine (旧{{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasya|qq=}}) |創建 = {{ill|647 AD|ja|647th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/647 AD|qq=}} (3rd year of {{ill|Taika|ja|大化|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taika|qq=}}) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = A Sunday near {{ill|October|ja|10月|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October|qq=}} 10 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label={{ill|Moriyama Hachimangu|ja|守山八幡宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriyama Hachimangu|qq=}}|coordinates= {{coord2|35|2|45.7|N|138|56|18.6|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark={{ill|Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|qq=}}|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Moriyama Hachimangu''' (守山八幡宮, もりやまはちまんぐう) is a Shinto shrine located in Izunokuni City, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuoka Prefecture|qq=}}. It is one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} of Izu Province and a successor shrine of {{ill|Iwatokotake Shrine|ja|石徳高神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatokotake Shrine|qq=}} (also read as {{ill|Iwato Kotakejinja|ja|いわとこたけじんじゃ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato Kotakejinja|qq=}}) along with {{ill|Mugatsuka Shrine|ja|豆塚神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mugatsuka Shrine|qq=}}, which is situated in the western, Emma district across the {{ill|Kano River|ja|狩野川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kano River|qq=}}. It is also known as the shrine where {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoritomo|qq=}} rallied his forces and prayed for the defeat of the Taira clan.(Shrine legend document)[https://izunotabi.com/sightseeing/moriyama-hachimangu/ Moriyama Hachimangu] - {{ill|Izunokuni City Tourism Association|ja|伊豆の国市観光協会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunokuni City Tourism Association|qq=}}[https://www.surugawan.net/guide/333.html Moriyama Hachimangu] - {{ill|Izu Suruga Tourism Guide|ja|伊豆駿河観光ガイド|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izu Suruga Tourism Guide|qq=}} == Overview == The shrine is located in the mountainous area of Moriyama, in the western part of the Terake district (formerly known as {{ill|Nirayama Town|ja|韮山町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nirayama Town|qq=}}) of Izunokuni City. This area is home to a concentration of historical sites related to the {{ill|Hōjō family|ja|北条家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōjō family|qq=}} and {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoritomo|qq=}}, as well as remains of the 【伝堀越御所跡】 from the Muromachi period. The shrine grounds are arranged in two levels. Immediately after ascending a flight of stone steps, one encounters the stage hall and the haiden. Beyond these, an additional 142 steep stone steps lead to the main hall and another haiden. At the lower stage hall, during the annual grand festival in October, rituals such as the Shagiri and the offering of the "Sambansō" are performed.[https://www.city.izunokuni.shizuoka.jp/tosikei/documents/nisyu32.pdf Moriyama Hachimangu and the Grand Festival] - Izunokuni City Urban Planning Division[https://www.city.izunokuni.shizuoka.jp/tosikei/documents/2-7nirayama-oohito.pdf Historical Aesthetics of Festival Rituals and Sambansō in the Nirayama-Oohito Area] - Izunokuni City Urban Planning Division == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|647 AD|ja|647th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/647 AD|qq=}} (the 3rd year of {{ill|Taika|ja|大化|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taika|qq=}}), enshrining {{ill|Ōyama Tsumi-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyama Tsumi-no-Kami|qq=}}. It later became a Shikinaisha in Izu Province through its association with Iwatokotake Shrine. In {{ill|907 AD|ja|907th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/907 AD|qq=}} (the 7th year of {{ill|Engi|ja|延喜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi|qq=}}), the deity {{ill|Hachimangu|ja|八幡神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachimangu|qq=}} was enshrined by invitation from {{ill|Usa Shrine|ja|宇佐神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Shrine|qq=}} of Buzen Province and merged with the shrine, which then became known as Moriyama Hachimangu. In August {{ill|1180 AD|ja|1180th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1180 AD|qq=}} (the 4th year of {{ill|Jishō|ja|治承|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jishō|qq=}}), when {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoritomo|qq=}} prayed for the defeat of the Taira clan and rallied his forces, several dozen of his soldiers attacked the mansion of {{ill|Yamaki Kanetaka|ja|山木兼隆|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaki Kanetaka|qq=}}, located near what would later become the Nirayama Daikan-sho, and the ensuing flames were observed from this site. The current main hall was constructed in {{ill|1632 AD|ja|1632nd year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1632 AD|qq=}} (the 9th year of {{ill|Kannen|ja|寛永|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannen|qq=}}) by Sakakibara Teruhisa, the lord of {{ill|Kunō Castle|ja|久能城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunō Castle|qq=}}, and replaced an earlier structure from 1597 (the 2nd year of {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keichō|qq=}}) by the lord of Nirayama Castle, Naitō Nobunari.[https://www.city.izunokuni.shizuoka.jp/tosikei/documents/2-4houjounosato-huuti.pdf Historical Aesthetics along the Old Shimoda Kaidō, as seen in Izunokuni City] - Izunokuni City Urban Planning Division The stage hall was constructed in {{ill|1934 AD|ja|1934th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1934 AD|qq=}} (Showa 9). == Deities == * {{ill|Hota Wakabetsu-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hota Wakabetsu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōyama Tsumi-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyama Tsumi-no-Kami|qq=}} * {{ill|Konohananosakuyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohananosakuyahime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}} == Grounds == The shrine grounds include: * Main Hall and Haiden * Stage Hall and its accompanying Haiden * A monument commemorating {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoritomo|qq=}}’s military expedition * Torii == Footnotes == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Moriyama-hachimangu (Izunokuni)}} * [https://izunotabi.com/sightseeing/moriyama-hachimangu/] - {{ill|Izunokuni City Tourism Association|ja|伊豆の国市観光協会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunokuni City Tourism Association|qq=}} * [https://www.surugawan.net/guide/333.html] - {{ill|Izu Suruga Tourism Guide|ja|伊豆駿河観光ガイド|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izu Suruga Tourism Guide|qq=}} {{神道 横}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|守山八幡宮 (伊豆の国市)|version=103447121|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Izunokuni City]] [[Category:Architectural Structures in Izunokuni City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachimangu]] }} [[sn:Moriyama Hachimangu (Izunokuni City)]] [[ja:守山八幡宮 (伊豆の国市)]] [[ja:守山八幡宮]] [[Category:Orphaned pages as of May 10, 2025]] [[Category:Orphaned pages as of May 10, 2025]] [[Category:Orphaned pages as of May 10, 2025]]

Former Village Shrine (旧{{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasya|qq=}}) |創建 = [[:en:647|647 AD]] (3rd year of [[:en:Taika (era)|Taika]]) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = A Sunday near [[:en:October|October]] 10 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label={{ill|Moriyama Hachimangu|ja|守山八幡宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriyama Hachimangu|qq=}}|coordinates= {{coord2|35|2|45.7|N|138|56|18.6|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=[[:en:Torii|Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg]]|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Moriyama Hachimangu''' (守山八幡宮, もりやまはちまんぐう) is a Shinto shrine located in Izunokuni City, [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]. It is one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} of Izu Province and a successor shrine of {{ill|Iwatokotake Shrine|ja|石徳高神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatokotake Shrine|qq=}} (also read as {{ill|Iwato Kotakejinja|ja|いわとこたけじんじゃ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato Kotakejinja|qq=}}) along with {{ill|Mugatsuka Shrine|ja|豆塚神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mugatsuka Shrine|qq=}}, which is situated in the western, Emma district across the [[:en:Kano River|Kano River]]. It is also known as the shrine where [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] rallied his forces and prayed for the defeat of the Taira clan.(Shrine legend document)[https://izunotabi.com/sightseeing/moriyama-hachimangu/ Moriyama Hachimangu] - {{ill|Izunokuni City Tourism Association|ja|伊豆の国市観光協会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunokuni City Tourism Association|qq=}}[https://www.surugawan.net/guide/333.html Moriyama Hachimangu] - {{ill|Izu Suruga Tourism Guide|ja|伊豆駿河観光ガイド|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izu Suruga Tourism Guide|qq=}} == Overview == The shrine is located in the mountainous area of Moriyama, in the western part of the Terake district (formerly known as [[:en:Nirayama, Shizuoka|Nirayama Town]]) of Izunokuni City. This area is home to a concentration of historical sites related to the {{ill|Hōjō family|ja|北条家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōjō family|qq=}} and [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], as well as remains of the 【伝堀越御所跡】 from the Muromachi period. The shrine grounds are arranged in two levels. Immediately after ascending a flight of stone steps, one encounters the stage hall and the haiden. Beyond these, an additional 142 steep stone steps lead to the main hall and another haiden. At the lower stage hall, during the annual grand festival in October, rituals such as the Shagiri and the offering of the "Sambansō" are performed.[https://www.city.izunokuni.shizuoka.jp/tosikei/documents/nisyu32.pdf Moriyama Hachimangu and the Grand Festival] - Izunokuni City Urban Planning Division[https://www.city.izunokuni.shizuoka.jp/tosikei/documents/2-7nirayama-oohito.pdf Historical Aesthetics of Festival Rituals and Sambansō in the Nirayama-Oohito Area] - Izunokuni City Urban Planning Division == History == The shrine was founded in [[:en:647|647 AD]] (the 3rd year of [[:en:Taika (era)|Taika]]), enshrining {{ill|Ōyama Tsumi-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyama Tsumi-no-Kami|qq=}}. It later became a Shikinaisha in Izu Province through its association with Iwatokotake Shrine. In [[:en:907|907 AD]] (the 7th year of [[:en:Engi (era)|Engi]]), the deity [[:en:Hachiman|Hachimangu]] was enshrined by invitation from [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Shrine]] of Buzen Province and merged with the shrine, which then became known as Moriyama Hachimangu. In August [[:en:1180|1180 AD]] (the 4th year of [[:en:Jishō|Jishō]]), when [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] prayed for the defeat of the Taira clan and rallied his forces, several dozen of his soldiers attacked the mansion of {{ill|Yamaki Kanetaka|ja|山木兼隆|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaki Kanetaka|qq=}}, located near what would later become the Nirayama Daikan-sho, and the ensuing flames were observed from this site. The current main hall was constructed in [[:en:1632|1632 AD]] (the 9th year of [[:en:Kan'ei|Kannen]]) by Sakakibara Teruhisa, the lord of {{ill|Kunō Castle|ja|久能城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunō Castle|qq=}}, and replaced an earlier structure from 1597 (the 2nd year of [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]]) by the lord of Nirayama Castle, Naitō Nobunari.[https://www.city.izunokuni.shizuoka.jp/tosikei/documents/2-4houjounosato-huuti.pdf Historical Aesthetics along the Old Shimoda Kaidō, as seen in Izunokuni City] - Izunokuni City Urban Planning Division The stage hall was constructed in [[:en:1934|1934 AD]] (Showa 9). == Deities == * {{ill|Hota Wakabetsu-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hota Wakabetsu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōyama Tsumi-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyama Tsumi-no-Kami|qq=}} * {{ill|Konohananosakuyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohananosakuyahime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}} == Grounds == The shrine grounds include: * Main Hall and Haiden * Stage Hall and its accompanying Haiden * A monument commemorating [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]]’s military expedition * Torii == Footnotes == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Moriyama-hachimangu (Izunokuni)}} * [https://izunotabi.com/sightseeing/moriyama-hachimangu/] - {{ill|Izunokuni City Tourism Association|ja|伊豆の国市観光協会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunokuni City Tourism Association|qq=}} * [https://www.surugawan.net/guide/333.html] - {{ill|Izu Suruga Tourism Guide|ja|伊豆駿河観光ガイド|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izu Suruga Tourism Guide|qq=}} {{神道 横}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|守山八幡宮 (伊豆の国市)|version=103447121|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Izunokuni City]] [[Category:Architectural Structures in Izunokuni City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachimangu]] }} [[sn:Moriyama Hachimangu (Izunokuni City)]] [[ja:守山八幡宮 (伊豆の国市)]] [[ja:守山八幡宮]] [[Category:伊豆の国市の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆の国市の歴史]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|2|45.7|N|138|56|18.6|E}}}} |所在地 = [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]][[:en:Izunokuni|Izunokuni City]], Terake 1204-1[{{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|守山八幡宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine|qq=}}] - {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Agency|ja|静岡県神社庁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Agency|qq=}} |位置 = {{Coord|35|2|45.7|N|138|56|18.6|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|Hota Wakabetsu-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hota Wakabetsu-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōyama Tsumi-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyama Tsumi-no-Kami|qq=}}, {{ill|Konohananosakuyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohananosakuyahime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}} |社格 = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}
Former Village Shrine (旧{{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasya|qq=}}) |創建 = [[:en:647|647 AD]] (3rd year of [[:en:Taika (era)|Taika]]) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = A Sunday near [[:en:October|October]] 10 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label={{ill|Moriyama Hachimangu|ja|守山八幡宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriyama Hachimangu|qq=}}|coordinates= {{coord2|35|2|45.7|N|138|56|18.6|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=[[:en:Torii|Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg]]|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Moriyama Hachimangu''' (守山八幡宮, もりやまはちまんぐう) is a Shinto shrine located in Izunokuni City, [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]. It is one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} of Izu Province and a successor shrine of {{ill|Iwatokotake Shrine|ja|石徳高神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatokotake Shrine|qq=}} (also read as {{ill|Iwato Kotakejinja|ja|いわとこたけじんじゃ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato Kotakejinja|qq=}}) along with {{ill|Mugatsuka Shrine|ja|豆塚神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mugatsuka Shrine|qq=}}, which is situated in the western, Emma district across the [[:en:Kano River|Kano River]]. It is also known as the shrine where [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] rallied his forces and prayed for the defeat of the Taira clan.(Shrine legend document)[https://izunotabi.com/sightseeing/moriyama-hachimangu/ Moriyama Hachimangu] - {{ill|Izunokuni City Tourism Association|ja|伊豆の国市観光協会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunokuni City Tourism Association|qq=}}[https://www.surugawan.net/guide/333.html Moriyama Hachimangu] - {{ill|Izu Suruga Tourism Guide|ja|伊豆駿河観光ガイド|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izu Suruga Tourism Guide|qq=}} == Overview == The shrine is located in the mountainous area of Moriyama, in the western part of the Terake district (formerly known as [[:en:Nirayama, Shizuoka|Nirayama Town]]) of Izunokuni City. This area is home to a concentration of historical sites related to the {{ill|Hōjō family|ja|北条家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōjō family|qq=}} and [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], as well as remains of the 【伝堀越御所跡】 from the Muromachi period. The shrine grounds are arranged in two levels. Immediately after ascending a flight of stone steps, one encounters the stage hall and the haiden. Beyond these, an additional 142 steep stone steps lead to the main hall and another haiden. At the lower stage hall, during the annual grand festival in October, rituals such as the Shagiri and the offering of the "Sambansō" are performed.[https://www.city.izunokuni.shizuoka.jp/tosikei/documents/nisyu32.pdf Moriyama Hachimangu and the Grand Festival] - Izunokuni City Urban Planning Division[https://www.city.izunokuni.shizuoka.jp/tosikei/documents/2-7nirayama-oohito.pdf Historical Aesthetics of Festival Rituals and Sambansō in the Nirayama-Oohito Area] - Izunokuni City Urban Planning Division == History == The shrine was founded in [[:en:647|647 AD]] (the 3rd year of [[:en:Taika (era)|Taika]]), enshrining {{ill|Ōyama Tsumi-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyama Tsumi-no-Kami|qq=}}. It later became a Shikinaisha in Izu Province through its association with Iwatokotake Shrine. In [[:en:907|907 AD]] (the 7th year of [[:en:Engi (era)|Engi]]), the deity [[:en:Hachiman|Hachimangu]] was enshrined by invitation from [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Shrine]] of Buzen Province and merged with the shrine, which then became known as Moriyama Hachimangu. In August [[:en:1180|1180 AD]] (the 4th year of [[:en:Jishō|Jishō]]), when [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] prayed for the defeat of the Taira clan and rallied his forces, several dozen of his soldiers attacked the mansion of {{ill|Yamaki Kanetaka|ja|山木兼隆|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaki Kanetaka|qq=}}, located near what would later become the Nirayama Daikan-sho, and the ensuing flames were observed from this site. The current main hall was constructed in [[:en:1632|1632 AD]] (the 9th year of [[:en:Kan'ei|Kannen]]) by Sakakibara Teruhisa, the lord of {{ill|Kunō Castle|ja|久能城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunō Castle|qq=}}, and replaced an earlier structure from 1597 (the 2nd year of [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]]) by the lord of Nirayama Castle, Naitō Nobunari.[https://www.city.izunokuni.shizuoka.jp/tosikei/documents/2-4houjounosato-huuti.pdf Historical Aesthetics along the Old Shimoda Kaidō, as seen in Izunokuni City] - Izunokuni City Urban Planning Division The stage hall was constructed in [[:en:1934|1934 AD]] (Showa 9). == Deities == * {{ill|Hota Wakabetsu-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hota Wakabetsu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōyama Tsumi-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyama Tsumi-no-Kami|qq=}} * {{ill|Konohananosakuyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohananosakuyahime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}} == Grounds == The shrine grounds include: * Main Hall and Haiden * Stage Hall and its accompanying Haiden * A monument commemorating [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]]’s military expedition * Torii == Footnotes == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Moriyama-hachimangu (Izunokuni)}} * [https://izunotabi.com/sightseeing/moriyama-hachimangu/] - {{ill|Izunokuni City Tourism Association|ja|伊豆の国市観光協会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunokuni City Tourism Association|qq=}} * [https://www.surugawan.net/guide/333.html] - {{ill|Izu Suruga Tourism Guide|ja|伊豆駿河観光ガイド|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izu Suruga Tourism Guide|qq=}} {{神道 横}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|守山八幡宮 (伊豆の国市)|version=103447121|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Izunokuni City]] [[Category:Architectural Structures in Izunokuni City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachimangu]] }} [[sn:Moriyama Hachimangu (Izunokuni City)]] [[ja:守山八幡宮 (伊豆の国市)]] [[ja:守山八幡宮]] [[Category:伊豆の国市の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆の国市の歴史]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]]

At the lower stage hall, during the annual grand festival in October, rituals such as the Shagiri and the offering of the "Sambansō" are performed.[https://www.city.izunokuni.shizuoka.jp/tosikei/documents/nisyu32.pdf Moriyama Hachimangu and the Grand Festival] - Izunokuni City Urban Planning Division[https://www.city.izunokuni.shizuoka.jp/tosikei/documents/2-7nirayama-oohito.pdf Historical Aesthetics of Festival Rituals and Sambansō in the Nirayama-Oohito Area] - Izunokuni City Urban Planning Division == History == The shrine was founded in [[:en:647|647 AD]] (the 3rd year of [[:en:Taika (era)|Taika]]), enshrining {{ill|Ōyama Tsumi-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyama Tsumi-no-Kami|qq=}}. It later became a Shikinaisha in Izu Province through its association with Iwatokotake Shrine. In [[:en:907|907 AD]] (the 7th year of [[:en:Engi (era)|Engi]]), the deity [[:en:Hachiman|Hachimangu]] was enshrined by invitation from [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Shrine]] of Buzen Province and merged with the shrine, which then became known as Moriyama Hachimangu. In August [[:en:1180|1180 AD]] (the 4th year of [[:en:Jishō|Jishō]]), when [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] prayed for the defeat of the Taira clan and rallied his forces, several dozen of his soldiers attacked the mansion of {{ill|Yamaki Kanetaka|ja|山木兼隆|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaki Kanetaka|qq=}}, located near what would later become the Nirayama Daikan-sho, and the ensuing flames were observed from this site. The current main hall was constructed in [[:en:1632|1632 AD]] (the 9th year of [[:en:Kan'ei|Kannen]]) by Sakakibara Teruhisa, the lord of {{ill|Kunō Castle|ja|久能城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunō Castle|qq=}}, and replaced an earlier structure from 1597 (the 2nd year of [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]]) by the lord of Nirayama Castle, Naitō Nobunari.[https://www.city.izunokuni.shizuoka.jp/tosikei/documents/2-4houjounosato-huuti.pdf Historical Aesthetics along the Old Shimoda Kaidō, as seen in Izunokuni City] - Izunokuni City Urban Planning Division The stage hall was constructed in [[:en:1934|1934 AD]] (Showa 9). == Deities == * {{ill|Hota Wakabetsu-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hota Wakabetsu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōyama Tsumi-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyama Tsumi-no-Kami|qq=}} * {{ill|Konohananosakuyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohananosakuyahime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}} == Grounds == The shrine grounds include: * Main Hall and Haiden * Stage Hall and its accompanying Haiden * A monument commemorating [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]]’s military expedition * Torii == Footnotes == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Moriyama-hachimangu (Izunokuni)}} * [https://izunotabi.com/sightseeing/moriyama-hachimangu/] - Former Village Shrine (旧{{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasya|qq=}}) |創建 = [[:en:647|647 AD]] (3rd year of [[:en:Taika (era)|Taika]]) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = A Sunday near [[:en:October|October]] 10 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label={{ill|Moriyama Hachimangu|ja|守山八幡宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriyama Hachimangu|qq=}}|coordinates= {{coord2|35|2|45.7|N|138|56|18.6|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=[[:en:Torii|Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg]]|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Moriyama Hachimangu''' (守山八幡宮, もりやまはちまんぐう) is a Shinto shrine located in Izunokuni City, [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]. It is one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} of Izu Province and a successor shrine of {{ill|Iwatokotake Shrine|ja|石徳高神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatokotake Shrine|qq=}} (also read as {{ill|Iwato Kotakejinja|ja|いわとこたけじんじゃ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato Kotakejinja|qq=}}) along with {{ill|Mugatsuka Shrine|ja|豆塚神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mugatsuka Shrine|qq=}}, which is situated in the western, Emma district across the [[:en:Kano River|Kano River]]. It is also known as the shrine where [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] rallied his forces and prayed for the defeat of the Taira clan.(Shrine legend document)[https://izunotabi.com/sightseeing/moriyama-hachimangu/ Moriyama Hachimangu] - {{ill|Izunokuni City Tourism Association|ja|伊豆の国市観光協会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunokuni City Tourism Association|qq=}}[https://www.surugawan.net/guide/333.html Moriyama Hachimangu] - {{ill|Izu Suruga Tourism Guide|ja|伊豆駿河観光ガイド|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izu Suruga Tourism Guide|qq=}} == Overview == The shrine is located in the mountainous area of Moriyama, in the western part of the Terake district (formerly known as [[:en:Nirayama, Shizuoka|Nirayama Town]]) of Izunokuni City. This area is home to a concentration of historical sites related to the {{ill|Hōjō family|ja|北条家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōjō family|qq=}} and [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], as well as remains of the 【伝堀越御所跡】 from the Muromachi period. The shrine grounds are arranged in two levels. Immediately after ascending a flight of stone steps, one encounters the stage hall and the haiden. Beyond these, an additional 142 steep stone steps lead to the main hall and another haiden. At the lower stage hall, during the annual grand festival in October, rituals such as the Shagiri and the offering of the "Sambansō" are performed.[https://www.city.izunokuni.shizuoka.jp/tosikei/documents/nisyu32.pdf Moriyama Hachimangu and the Grand Festival] - Izunokuni City Urban Planning Division[https://www.city.izunokuni.shizuoka.jp/tosikei/documents/2-7nirayama-oohito.pdf Historical Aesthetics of Festival Rituals and Sambansō in the Nirayama-Oohito Area] - Izunokuni City Urban Planning Division == History == The shrine was founded in [[:en:647|647 AD]] (the 3rd year of [[:en:Taika (era)|Taika]]), enshrining {{ill|Ōyama Tsumi-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyama Tsumi-no-Kami|qq=}}. In August [[:en:1180|1180 AD]] (the 4th year of [[:en:Jishō|Jishō]]), when [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] prayed for the defeat of the Taira clan and rallied his forces, several dozen of his soldiers attacked the mansion of {{ill|Yamaki Kanetaka|ja|山木兼隆|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaki Kanetaka|qq=}}, located near what would later become the Nirayama Daikan-sho, and the ensuing flames were observed from this site. The current main hall was constructed in [[:en:1632|1632 AD]] (the 9th year of [[:en:Kan'ei|Kannen]]) by Sakakibara Teruhisa, the lord of {{ill|Kunō Castle|ja|久能城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunō Castle|qq=}}, and replaced an earlier structure from 1597 (the 2nd year of [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]]) by the lord of Nirayama Castle, Naitō Nobunari.[https://www.city.izunokuni.shizuoka.jp/tosikei/documents/2-4houjounosato-huuti.pdf Historical Aesthetics along the Old Shimoda Kaidō, as seen in Izunokuni City] - [[commons:Category:Moriyama-hachimangu (Izunokuni)]][[ja:守山八幡宮 (伊豆の国市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q116968871]] Wikidata name: Moriyama-hachimangu (Izunokuni) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |名称 = {{ill|Moriyama Hachimangu|ja|守山八幡宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriyama Hachimangu|qq=|1=Moriyama Hachimangu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110857068}} |image = File:Moriyama-hachimangu (Izunokuni), torii-1.jpg |所在地 = [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]][[:en:Izunokuni|Izunokuni City]], Terake 1204-1[{{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|守山八幡宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine|qq=}}] - {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Agency|ja|静岡県神社庁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Agency|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社庁|1=Jinja-cho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590703}} |位置 = {{Coord|35|2|45.7|N|138|56|18.6|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|Hota Wakabetsu-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hota Wakabetsu-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|1=Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}}, {{ill|Ōyama Tsumi-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyama Tsumi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q386563}}, {{ill|Konohananosakuyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohananosakuyahime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} |社格 = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}
Former Village Shrine (旧{{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}) |創建 = [[:en:647|647 AD]] (3rd year of [[:en:Taika (era)|Taika]]) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = A Sunday near [[:en:October|October]] 10 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label={{ill|Moriyama Hachimangu|ja|守山八幡宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriyama Hachimangu|qq=|1=Moriyama Hachimangu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110857068}}|coordinates= {{coord2|35|2|45.7|N|138|56|18.6|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=[[:en:Torii|Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg]]|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} |prefecture = {{ill|Izunokuni|qid=Q863758}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.04602777777778|N|138.9385|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Moriyama Hachimangu''' (守山八幡宮, もりやまはちまんぐう) is a Shinto shrine located in Izunokuni City, [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]. It is one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} of Izu Province and a successor shrine of {{ill|Iwatokotake Shrine|ja|石徳高神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatokotake Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also read as {{ill|Iwato Kotakejinja|ja|いわとこたけじんじゃ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato Kotakejinja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) along with {{ill|Mugatsuka Shrine|ja|豆塚神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mugatsuka Shrine|qq=|1=Mamezuka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q128867065}}, which is situated in the western, Emma district across the [[:en:Kano River|Kano River]]. It is also known as the shrine where [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] rallied his forces and prayed for the defeat of the Taira clan.(Shrine legend document)[https://izunotabi.com/sightseeing/moriyama-hachimangu/ Moriyama Hachimangu] - {{ill|Izunokuni City Tourism Association|ja|伊豆の国市観光協会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunokuni City Tourism Association|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}[https://www.surugawan.net/guide/333.html Moriyama Hachimangu] - {{ill|Izu Suruga Tourism Guide|ja|伊豆駿河観光ガイド|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izu Suruga Tourism Guide|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Overview == The shrine is located in the mountainous area of Moriyama, in the western part of the Terake district (formerly known as [[:en:Nirayama, Shizuoka|Nirayama Town]]) of Izunokuni City. This area is home to a concentration of historical sites related to the {{ill|Hōjō family|ja|北条家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōjō family|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 北条氏 (曖昧さ回避)|1=Hojo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q398711}} and [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], as well as remains of the 【伝堀越御所跡】 from the Muromachi period. The shrine grounds are arranged in two levels. Immediately after ascending a flight of stone steps, one encounters the stage hall and the haiden. Beyond these, an additional 142 steep stone steps lead to the main hall and another haiden. At the lower stage hall, during the annual grand festival in October, rituals such as the Shagiri and the offering of the "Sambansō" are performed.[https://www.city.izunokuni.shizuoka.jp/tosikei/documents/nisyu32.pdf Moriyama Hachimangu and the Grand Festival] - Izunokuni City Urban Planning Division[https://www.city.izunokuni.shizuoka.jp/tosikei/documents/2-7nirayama-oohito.pdf Historical Aesthetics of Festival Rituals and Sambansō in the Nirayama-Oohito Area] - Izunokuni City Urban Planning Division == History == The shrine was founded in [[:en:647|647 AD]] (the 3rd year of [[:en:Taika (era)|Taika]]), enshrining {{ill|Ōyama Tsumi-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyama Tsumi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q386563}}. It later became a Shikinaisha in Izu Province through its association with Iwatokotake Shrine. In [[:en:907|907 AD]] (the 7th year of [[:en:Engi (era)|Engi]]), the deity [[:en:Hachiman|Hachimangu]] was enshrined by invitation from [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Shrine]] of Buzen Province and merged with the shrine, which then became known as Moriyama Hachimangu. In August [[:en:1180|1180 AD]] (the 4th year of [[:en:Jishō|Jishō]]), when [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] prayed for the defeat of the Taira clan and rallied his forces, several dozen of his soldiers attacked the mansion of {{ill|Yamaki Kanetaka|ja|山木兼隆|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaki Kanetaka|qq=|1=Yamaki Kanetaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11468747}}, located near what would later become the Nirayama Daikan-sho, and the ensuing flames were observed from this site. The current main hall was constructed in [[:en:1632|1632 AD]] (the 9th year of [[:en:Kan'ei|Kannen]]) by Sakakibara Teruhisa, the lord of {{ill|Kunō Castle|ja|久能城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunō Castle|qq=|1=Kunō Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65248350}}, and replaced an earlier structure from 1597 (the 2nd year of [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]]) by the lord of Nirayama Castle, Naitō Nobunari.[https://www.city.izunokuni.shizuoka.jp/tosikei/documents/2-4houjounosato-huuti.pdf Historical Aesthetics along the Old Shimoda Kaidō, as seen in Izunokuni City] - Izunokuni City Urban Planning Division The stage hall was constructed in [[:en:1934|1934 AD]] (Showa 9). == Deities == * {{ill|Hota Wakabetsu-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hota Wakabetsu-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|1=Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}} * {{ill|Ōyama Tsumi-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyama Tsumi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q386563}} * {{ill|Konohananosakuyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohananosakuyahime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} == Grounds == The shrine grounds include: * Main Hall and Haiden * Stage Hall and its accompanying Haiden * A monument commemorating [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]]’s military expedition * Torii == Footnotes == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Moriyama-hachimangu (Izunokuni)}} * [https://izunotabi.com/sightseeing/moriyama-hachimangu/] - {{ill|Izunokuni City Tourism Association|ja|伊豆の国市観光協会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunokuni City Tourism Association|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [https://www.surugawan.net/guide/333.html] - {{ill|Izu Suruga Tourism Guide|ja|伊豆駿河観光ガイド|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izu Suruga Tourism Guide|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|守山八幡宮 (伊豆の国市)|version=103447121|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] }} [[ja:守山八幡宮 (伊豆の国市)]] [[ja:守山八幡宮]] [[Category:Buildings in Izunokuni]] [[Category:History of Izunokuni]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Moriyama-hachimangu (Izunokuni)}} {{wikidata link|Q116968871}}

Former Village Shrine (旧{{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}) |創建 = [[:en:647|647 AD]] (3rd year of [[:en:Taika (era)|Taika]]) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = A Sunday near [[:en:October|October]] 10 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label={{ill|Moriyama Hachimangu|ja|守山八幡宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriyama Hachimangu|qq=|1=Moriyama Hachimangu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110857068}}|coordinates= {{coord2|35|2|45.7|N|138|56|18.6|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=[[:en:Torii|Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg]]|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} |prefecture = {{ill|Izunokuni|qid=Q863758}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.04602777777778|N|138.9385|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Moriyama Hachimangu''' (守山八幡宮, もりやまはちまんぐう) is a Shinto shrine located in Izunokuni City, [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]. It is one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} of Izu Province and a successor shrine of {{ill|Iwatokotake Shrine|ja|石徳高神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatokotake Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also read as {{ill|Iwato Kotakejinja|ja|いわとこたけじんじゃ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato Kotakejinja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) along with {{ill|Mugatsuka Shrine|ja|豆塚神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mugatsuka Shrine|qq=|1=Mamezuka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q128867065}}, which is situated in the western, Emma district across the [[:en:Kano River|Kano River]]. It is also known as the shrine where [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] rallied his forces and prayed for the defeat of the Taira clan.(Shrine legend document)[https://izunotabi.com/sightseeing/moriyama-hachimangu/ Moriyama Hachimangu] - {{ill|Izunokuni City Tourism Association|ja|伊豆の国市観光協会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunokuni City Tourism Association|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}[https://www.surugawan.net/guide/333.html Moriyama Hachimangu] - {{ill|Izu Suruga Tourism Guide|ja|伊豆駿河観光ガイド|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izu Suruga Tourism Guide|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Overview == The shrine is located in the mountainous area of Moriyama, in the western part of the Terake district (formerly known as [[:en:Nirayama, Shizuoka|Nirayama Town]]) of Izunokuni City. This area is home to a concentration of historical sites related to the {{ill|Hōjō family|ja|北条家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōjō family|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 北条氏 (曖昧さ回避)|1=Hojo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q398711}} and [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], as well as remains of the 【伝堀越御所跡】 from the Muromachi period. The shrine grounds are arranged in two levels. Immediately after ascending a flight of stone steps, one encounters the stage hall and the haiden. Beyond these, an additional 142 steep stone steps lead to the main hall and another haiden. At the lower stage hall, during the annual grand festival in October, rituals such as the Shagiri and the offering of the "Sambansō" are performed.[https://www.city.izunokuni.shizuoka.jp/tosikei/documents/nisyu32.pdf Moriyama Hachimangu and the Grand Festival] - Izunokuni City Urban Planning Division[https://www.city.izunokuni.shizuoka.jp/tosikei/documents/2-7nirayama-oohito.pdf Historical Aesthetics of Festival Rituals and Sambansō in the Nirayama-Oohito Area] - Izunokuni City Urban Planning Division == History == The shrine was founded in [[:en:647|647 AD]] (the 3rd year of [[:en:Taika (era)|Taika]]), enshrining {{ill|Ōyama Tsumi-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyama Tsumi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q386563}}. It later became a Shikinaisha in Izu Province through its association with Iwatokotake Shrine. In [[:en:907|907 AD]] (the 7th year of [[:en:Engi (era)|Engi]]), the deity [[:en:Hachiman|Hachimangu]] was enshrined by invitation from [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Shrine]] of Buzen Province and merged with the shrine, which then became known as Moriyama Hachimangu. In August [[:en:1180|1180 AD]] (the 4th year of [[:en:Jishō|Jishō]]), when [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] prayed for the defeat of the Taira clan and rallied his forces, several dozen of his soldiers attacked the mansion of {{ill|Yamaki Kanetaka|ja|山木兼隆|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaki Kanetaka|qq=|1=Yamaki Kanetaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11468747}}, located near what would later become the Nirayama Daikan-sho, and the ensuing flames were observed from this site. The current main hall was constructed in [[:en:1632|1632 AD]] (the 9th year of [[:en:Kan'ei|Kannen]]) by Sakakibara Teruhisa, the lord of {{ill|Kunō Castle|ja|久能城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunō Castle|qq=|1=Kunō Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65248350}}, and replaced an earlier structure from 1597 (the 2nd year of [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]]) by the lord of Nirayama Castle, Naitō Nobunari.[https://www.city.izunokuni.shizuoka.jp/tosikei/documents/2-4houjounosato-huuti.pdf Historical Aesthetics along the Old Shimoda Kaidō, as seen in Izunokuni City] - Izunokuni City Urban Planning Division The stage hall was constructed in [[:en:1934|1934 AD]] (Showa 9). == Deities == * {{ill|Hota Wakabetsu-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hota Wakabetsu-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|1=Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}} * {{ill|Ōyama Tsumi-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyama Tsumi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q386563}} * {{ill|Konohananosakuyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohananosakuyahime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} == Grounds == The shrine grounds include: * Main Hall and Haiden * Stage Hall and its accompanying Haiden * A monument commemorating [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]]’s military expedition * Torii == Footnotes == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Moriyama-hachimangu (Izunokuni)}} * [https://izunotabi.com/sightseeing/moriyama-hachimangu/] -
