{{Infobox religious building |名称 = {{ill|Moriyama Hachimangu|ja|守山八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriyama Hachimangu|1=Moriyama Hachimangu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q110857068}} |image = File:Moriyama-hachimangu (Izunokuni), torii-1.jpg |所在地 = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|qid=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|Izunokuni|en|Izunokuni|qid=Q863758|lt=Izunokuni City|1=Izunokuni}}, Terake 1204-1[{{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|守山八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine|qq=}}] - {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Agency|ja|静岡県神社庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Agency|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社庁|1=Jinjachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11590703}} |位置 = {{Coord|35|2|45.7|N|138|56|18.6|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|Hota Wakabetsu-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hota Wakabetsu-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|1=Emperor Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q317997}}, {{ill|Ōyama Tsumi-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyama Tsumi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q386563}}, {{ill|Konohananosakuyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohananosakuyahime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} |社格 = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}
Former Village Shrine (旧{{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}) |創建 = {{ill|647|en|647|qid=Q31111|lt=647 AD|1=647}} (3rd year of {{ill|Taika (era)|en|Taika (era)|qid=Q1077930|lt=Taika|1=Taika (era)}}) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = A Sunday near {{ill|October|en|October|qid=Q124|lt=October|1=October}} 10 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label={{ill|Moriyama Hachimangu|ja|守山八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriyama Hachimangu|1=Moriyama Hachimangu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q110857068}}|coordinates= {{coord2|35|2|45.7|N|138|56|18.6|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark={{ill|Torii|en|Torii|qid=Q234731|lt=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|1=Torii}}|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} |prefecture = {{ill|Izunokuni|qid=Q863758|1=Izunokuni}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.04602777777778|N|138.9385|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Moriyama Hachimangu''' (守山八幡宮, もりやまはちまんぐう) is a Shinto shrine located in Izunokuni City, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|qid=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It is one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} of Izu Province and a successor shrine of {{ill|Iwatokotake Shrine|ja|石徳高神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatokotake Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also read as {{ill|Iwato Kotakejinja|ja|いわとこたけじんじゃ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato Kotakejinja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) along with {{ill|Mugatsuka Shrine|ja|豆塚神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mugatsuka Shrine|1=Mamezuka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q128867065}}, which is situated in the western, Emma district across the {{ill|Kano River|en|Kano River|qid=Q6364188|lt=Kano River|1=Kano River}}. It is also known as the shrine where {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|qid=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} rallied his forces and prayed for the defeat of the Taira clan.(Shrine legend document)[https://izunotabi.com/sightseeing/moriyama-hachimangu/ Moriyama Hachimangu] - {{ill|Izunokuni City Tourism Association|ja|伊豆の国市観光協会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunokuni City Tourism Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}[https://www.surugawan.net/guide/333.html Moriyama Hachimangu] - {{ill|Izu Suruga Tourism Guide|ja|伊豆駿河観光ガイド|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izu Suruga Tourism Guide|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Overview == The shrine is located in the mountainous area of Moriyama, in the western part of the Terake district (formerly known as {{ill|Nirayama, Shizuoka|en|Nirayama, Shizuoka|qid=Q7039915|lt=Nirayama Town|1=Nirayama, Shizuoka}}) of Izunokuni City. This area is home to a concentration of historical sites related to the {{ill|Hōjō family|ja|北条家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōjō family|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 北条氏 (曖昧さ回避)|1=Hojo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q398711}} and {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|qid=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}}, as well as remains of the 【伝堀越御所跡】 from the Muromachi period. The shrine grounds are arranged in two levels. Immediately after ascending a flight of stone steps, one encounters the stage hall and the haiden. Beyond these, an additional 142 steep stone steps lead to the main hall and another haiden. At the lower stage hall, during the annual grand festival in October, rituals such as the Shagiri and the offering of the "Sambansō" are performed.[https://www.city.izunokuni.shizuoka.jp/tosikei/documents/nisyu32.pdf Moriyama Hachimangu and the Grand Festival] - Izunokuni City Urban Planning Division[https://www.city.izunokuni.shizuoka.jp/tosikei/documents/2-7nirayama-oohito.pdf Historical Aesthetics of Festival Rituals and Sambansō in the Nirayama-Oohito Area] - Izunokuni City Urban Planning Division == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|647|en|647|qid=Q31111|lt=647 AD|1=647}} (the 3rd year of {{ill|Taika (era)|en|Taika (era)|qid=Q1077930|lt=Taika|1=Taika (era)}}), enshrining {{ill|Ōyama Tsumi-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyama Tsumi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q386563}}. It later became a Shikinaisha in Izu Province through its association with Iwatokotake Shrine. In {{ill|907|en|907|qid=Q24163|lt=907 AD|1=907}} (the 7th year of {{ill|Engi (era)|en|Engi (era)|qid=Q1374325|lt=Engi|1=Engi (era)}}), the deity {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|qid=Q261637|lt=Hachimangu|1=Hachiman}} was enshrined by invitation from {{ill|Usa Jingū|en|Usa Jingū|qid=Q715632|lt=Usa Shrine|1=Usa Jingū}} of Buzen Province and merged with the shrine, which then became known as Moriyama Hachimangu. In August {{ill|1180|en|1180|qid=Q19702|lt=1180 AD|1=1180}} (the 4th year of {{ill|Jishō|en|Jishō|qid=Q1194671|lt=Jishō|1=Jishō}}), when {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|qid=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} prayed for the defeat of the Taira clan and rallied his forces, several dozen of his soldiers attacked the mansion of {{ill|Yamaki Kanetaka|ja|山木兼隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaki Kanetaka|1=Yamaki Kanetaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11468747}}, located near what would later become the Nirayama Daikan-sho, and the ensuing flames were observed from this site. The current main hall was constructed in {{ill|1632|en|1632|qid=Q6865|lt=1632 AD|1=1632}} (the 9th year of {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|qid=Q1192913|lt=Kannen|1=Kan'ei}}) by Sakakibara Teruhisa, the lord of {{ill|Kunō Castle|ja|久能城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunō Castle|1=Kunō Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q65248350}}, and replaced an earlier structure from 1597 (the 2nd year of {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|qid=Q1328185|lt=Keichō|1=Keichō}}) by the lord of Nirayama Castle, Naitō Nobunari.[https://www.city.izunokuni.shizuoka.jp/tosikei/documents/2-4houjounosato-huuti.pdf Historical Aesthetics along the Old Shimoda Kaidō, as seen in Izunokuni City] - Izunokuni City Urban Planning Division The stage hall was constructed in {{ill|1934|en|1934|qid=Q18714|lt=1934 AD|1=1934}} (Showa 9). == Deities == * {{ill|Hota Wakabetsu-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hota Wakabetsu-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|1=Emperor Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q317997}} * {{ill|Ōyama Tsumi-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyama Tsumi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q386563}} * {{ill|Konohananosakuyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohananosakuyahime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} == Grounds == The shrine grounds include: * Main Hall and Haiden * Stage Hall and its accompanying Haiden * A monument commemorating {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|qid=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}}’s military expedition * Torii == Footnotes == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Moriyama-hachimangu (Izunokuni)}} * [https://izunotabi.com/sightseeing/moriyama-hachimangu/] - {{ill|Izunokuni City Tourism Association|ja|伊豆の国市観光協会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunokuni City Tourism Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [https://www.surugawan.net/guide/333.html] - {{ill|Izu Suruga Tourism Guide|ja|伊豆駿河観光ガイド|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izu Suruga Tourism Guide|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|守山八幡宮 (伊豆の国市)|version=103447121|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Moriyama-hachimangu (Izunokuni)}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Moriyama Hachimangu (Izunokuni City) (Q116968871)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | RONSHA || {{ill|Moriyama Hachimangu (Izunokuni City)|lt=Moriyama-hachimangu (Izunokuni)|ja|守山八幡宮 (伊豆の国市)|commons|Category:Moriyama-hachimangu (Izunokuni)|qid=Q116968871|1=Moriyama Hachimangu (Izunokuni City)}} || — || — || — || {{ill|Ihatokono Shrine|lt=Ihatokono Shrine|qid=Q135039670|1=Ihatokono Shrine}} || {{coord|35.046028|138.938500|display=inline}} || — |} {{wikidata link|Q116968871}}==Notes== [[co:commons:Category:Moriyama-hachimangu (Izunokuni)]] [[da:d:Q116968871]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Izunokuni]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:History of Izunokuni]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]

Former Village Shrine (旧{{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}) |創建 = {{ill|647|en|647|qid=Q31111|lt=647 AD|1=647}} (3rd year of {{ill|Taika (era)|en|Taika (era)|qid=Q1077930|lt=Taika|1=Taika (era)}}) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = A Sunday near {{ill|October|en|October|qid=Q124|lt=October|1=October}} 10 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label={{ill|Moriyama Hachimangu|ja|守山八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriyama Hachimangu|1=Moriyama Hachimangu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q110857068}}|coordinates= {{coord2|35|2|45.7|N|138|56|18.6|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark={{ill|Torii|en|Torii|qid=Q234731|lt=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|1=Torii}}|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} |prefecture = {{ill|Izunokuni|qid=Q863758|1=Izunokuni}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.04602777777778|N|138.9385|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Moriyama Hachimangu''' (守山八幡宮, もりやまはちまんぐう) is a Shinto shrine located in Izunokuni City, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|qid=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It is one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} of Izu Province and a successor shrine of {{ill|Iwatokotake Shrine|ja|石徳高神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatokotake Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also read as {{ill|Iwato Kotakejinja|ja|いわとこたけじんじゃ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato Kotakejinja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) along with {{ill|Mugatsuka Shrine|ja|豆塚神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mugatsuka Shrine|1=Mamezuka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q128867065}}, which is situated in the western, Emma district across the {{ill|Kano River|en|Kano River|qid=Q6364188|lt=Kano River|1=Kano River}}. It is also known as the shrine where {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|qid=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} rallied his forces and prayed for the defeat of the Taira clan.(Shrine legend document)[https://izunotabi.com/sightseeing/moriyama-hachimangu/ Moriyama Hachimangu] - {{ill|Izunokuni City Tourism Association|ja|伊豆の国市観光協会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunokuni City Tourism Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}[https://www.surugawan.net/guide/333.html Moriyama Hachimangu] - {{ill|Izu Suruga Tourism Guide|ja|伊豆駿河観光ガイド|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izu Suruga Tourism Guide|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Overview == The shrine is located in the mountainous area of Moriyama, in the western part of the Terake district (formerly known as {{ill|Nirayama, Shizuoka|en|Nirayama, Shizuoka|qid=Q7039915|lt=Nirayama Town|1=Nirayama, Shizuoka}}) of Izunokuni City. This area is home to a concentration of historical sites related to the {{ill|Hōjō family|ja|北条家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōjō family|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 北条氏 (曖昧さ回避)|1=Hojo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q398711}} and {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|qid=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}}, as well as remains of the 【伝堀越御所跡】 from the Muromachi period. The shrine grounds are arranged in two levels. Immediately after ascending a flight of stone steps, one encounters the stage hall and the haiden. Beyond these, an additional 142 steep stone steps lead to the main hall and another haiden. At the lower stage hall, during the annual grand festival in October, rituals such as the Shagiri and the offering of the "Sambansō" are performed.[https://www.city.izunokuni.shizuoka.jp/tosikei/documents/nisyu32.pdf Moriyama Hachimangu and the Grand Festival] - Izunokuni City Urban Planning Division[https://www.city.izunokuni.shizuoka.jp/tosikei/documents/2-7nirayama-oohito.pdf Historical Aesthetics of Festival Rituals and Sambansō in the Nirayama-Oohito Area] - Izunokuni City Urban Planning Division == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|647|en|647|qid=Q31111|lt=647 AD|1=647}} (the 3rd year of {{ill|Taika (era)|en|Taika (era)|qid=Q1077930|lt=Taika|1=Taika (era)}}), enshrining {{ill|Ōyama Tsumi-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyama Tsumi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q386563}}. It later became a Shikinaisha in Izu Province through its association with Iwatokotake Shrine. In {{ill|907|en|907|qid=Q24163|lt=907 AD|1=907}} (the 7th year of {{ill|Engi (era)|en|Engi (era)|qid=Q1374325|lt=Engi|1=Engi (era)}}), the deity {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|qid=Q261637|lt=Hachimangu|1=Hachiman}} was enshrined by invitation from {{ill|Usa Jingū|en|Usa Jingū|qid=Q715632|lt=Usa Shrine|1=Usa Jingū}} of Buzen Province and merged with the shrine, which then became known as Moriyama Hachimangu. In August {{ill|1180|en|1180|qid=Q19702|lt=1180 AD|1=1180}} (the 4th year of {{ill|Jishō|en|Jishō|qid=Q1194671|lt=Jishō|1=Jishō}}), when {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|qid=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} prayed for the defeat of the Taira clan and rallied his forces, several dozen of his soldiers attacked the mansion of {{ill|Yamaki Kanetaka|ja|山木兼隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaki Kanetaka|1=Yamaki Kanetaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11468747}}, located near what would later become the Nirayama Daikan-sho, and the ensuing flames were observed from this site. The current main hall was constructed in {{ill|1632|en|1632|qid=Q6865|lt=1632 AD|1=1632}} (the 9th year of {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|qid=Q1192913|lt=Kannen|1=Kan'ei}}) by Sakakibara Teruhisa, the lord of {{ill|Kunō Castle|ja|久能城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunō Castle|1=Kunō Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q65248350}}, and replaced an earlier structure from 1597 (the 2nd year of {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|qid=Q1328185|lt=Keichō|1=Keichō}}) by the lord of Nirayama Castle, Naitō Nobunari.[https://www.city.izunokuni.shizuoka.jp/tosikei/documents/2-4houjounosato-huuti.pdf Historical Aesthetics along the Old Shimoda Kaidō, as seen in Izunokuni City] - Izunokuni City Urban Planning Division The stage hall was constructed in {{ill|1934|en|1934|qid=Q18714|lt=1934 AD|1=1934}} (Showa 9). == Deities == * {{ill|Hota Wakabetsu-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hota Wakabetsu-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|1=Emperor Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q317997}} * {{ill|Ōyama Tsumi-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyama Tsumi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q386563}} * {{ill|Konohananosakuyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohananosakuyahime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} == Grounds == The shrine grounds include: * Main Hall and Haiden * Stage Hall and its accompanying Haiden * A monument commemorating {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|qid=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}}’s military expedition * Torii == Footnotes == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Moriyama-hachimangu (Izunokuni)}} * [https://izunotabi.com/sightseeing/moriyama-hachimangu/] - {{ill|Izunokuni City Tourism Association|ja|伊豆の国市観光協会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunokuni City Tourism Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [https://www.surugawan.net/guide/333.html] - {{ill|Izu Suruga Tourism Guide|ja|伊豆駿河観光ガイド|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izu Suruga Tourism Guide|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|守山八幡宮 (伊豆の国市)|version=103447121|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Moriyama-hachimangu (Izunokuni)}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] '''Moriyama Hachimangu (Izunokuni City) (Q116968871)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | RONSHA || {{ill|Moriyama Hachimangu (Izunokuni City)|lt=Moriyama-hachimangu (Izunokuni)|ja|守山八幡宮 (伊豆の国市)|commons|Category:Moriyama-hachimangu (Izunokuni)|qid=Q116968871|1=Moriyama Hachimangu (Izunokuni City)}} || — || — || — || {{ill|Ihatokono Shrine|lt=Ihatokono Shrine|qid=Q135039670|1=Ihatokono Shrine}} || {{coord|35.046028|138.938500|display=inline}} || — |} {{wikidata link|Q116968871}}==Notes== [[co:commons:Category:Moriyama-hachimangu (Izunokuni)]] [[da:d:Q116968871]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Izunokuni]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:History of Izunokuni]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]

It is one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} of Izu Province and a successor shrine of {{ill|Iwatokotake Shrine|ja|石徳高神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatokotake Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also read as {{ill|Iwato Kotakejinja|ja|いわとこたけじんじゃ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato Kotakejinja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) along with {{ill|Mugatsuka Shrine|ja|豆塚神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mugatsuka Shrine|1=Mamezuka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q128867065}}, which is situated in the western, Emma district across the {{ill|Kano River|en|Kano River|qid=Q6364188|lt=Kano River|1=Kano River}}. It is also known as the shrine where {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|qid=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} rallied his forces and prayed for the defeat of the Taira clan.(Shrine legend document)[https://izunotabi.com/sightseeing/moriyama-hachimangu/ Moriyama Hachimangu] - {{ill|Izunokuni City Tourism Association|ja|伊豆の国市観光協会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunokuni City Tourism Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}[https://www.surugawan.net/guide/333.html Moriyama Hachimangu] - {{ill|Izu Suruga Tourism Guide|ja|伊豆駿河観光ガイド|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izu Suruga Tourism Guide|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Overview == The shrine is located in the mountainous area of Moriyama, in the western part of the Terake district (formerly known as {{ill|Nirayama, Shizuoka|en|Nirayama, Shizuoka|qid=Q7039915|lt=Nirayama Town|1=Nirayama, Shizuoka}}) of Izunokuni City. The shrine grounds are arranged in two levels. Immediately after ascending a flight of stone steps, one encounters the stage hall and the haiden. Beyond these, an additional 142 steep stone steps lead to the main hall and another haiden. At the lower stage hall, during the annual grand festival in October, rituals such as the Shagiri and the offering of the "Sambansō" are performed.[https://www.city.izunokuni.shizuoka.jp/tosikei/documents/nisyu32.pdf Moriyama Hachimangu and the Grand Festival] - Izunokuni City Urban Planning Division[https://www.city.izunokuni.shizuoka.jp/tosikei/documents/2-7nirayama-oohito.pdf Historical Aesthetics of Festival Rituals and Sambansō in the Nirayama-Oohito Area] - Izunokuni City Urban Planning Division == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|647|en|647|qid=Q31111|lt=647 AD|1=647}} (the 3rd year of {{ill|Taika (era)|en|Taika (era)|qid=Q1077930|lt=Taika|1=Taika (era)}}), enshrining {{ill|Ōyama Tsumi-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyama Tsumi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q386563}}. It later became a Shikinaisha in Izu Province through its association with Iwatokotake Shrine. In {{ill|907|en|907|qid=Q24163|lt=907 AD|1=907}} (the 7th year of {{ill|Engi (era)|en|Engi (era)|qid=Q1374325|lt=Engi|1=Engi (era)}}), the deity {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|qid=Q261637|lt=Hachimangu|1=Hachiman}} was enshrined by invitation from {{ill|Usa Jingū|en|Usa Jingū|qid=Q715632|lt=Usa Shrine|1=Usa Jingū}} of Buzen Province and merged with the shrine, which then became known as Moriyama Hachimangu. In August {{ill|1180|en|1180|qid=Q19702|lt=1180 AD|1=1180}} (the 4th year of {{ill|Jishō|en|Jishō|qid=Q1194671|lt=Jishō|1=Jishō}}), when {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|qid=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} prayed for the defeat of the Taira clan and rallied his forces, several dozen of his soldiers attacked the mansion of {{ill|Yamaki Kanetaka|ja|山木兼隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaki Kanetaka|1=Yamaki Kanetaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11468747}}, located near what would later become the Nirayama Daikan-sho, and the ensuing flames were observed from this site. The current main hall was constructed in {{ill|1632|en|1632|qid=Q6865|lt=1632 AD|1=1632}} (the 9th year of {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|qid=Q1192913|lt=Kannen|1=Kan'ei}}) by Sakakibara Teruhisa, the lord of {{ill|Kunō Castle|ja|久能城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunō Castle|1=Kunō Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q65248350}}, and replaced an earlier structure from 1597 (the 2nd year of {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|qid=Q1328185|lt=Keichō|1=Keichō}}) by the lord of Nirayama Castle, Naitō Nobunari.[https://www.city.izunokuni.shizuoka.jp/tosikei/documents/2-4houjounosato-huuti.pdf Historical Aesthetics along the Old Shimoda Kaidō, as seen in Izunokuni City] - Izunokuni City Urban Planning Division The stage hall was constructed in {{ill|1934|en|1934|qid=Q18714|lt=1934 AD|1=1934}} (Showa 9). == Deities == * {{ill|Hota Wakabetsu-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hota Wakabetsu-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|1=Emperor Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q317997}} * {{ill|Ōyama Tsumi-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyama Tsumi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q386563}} * {{ill|Konohananosakuyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohananosakuyahime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} == Grounds == The shrine grounds include: * Main Hall and Haiden * Stage Hall and its accompanying Haiden * A monument commemorating {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|qid=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}}’s military expedition * Torii == Footnotes == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Moriyama-hachimangu (Izunokuni)}} * [https://izunotabi.com/sightseeing/moriyama-hachimangu/] -
